title: Produce + translate additional objects across ≥2 target languages
context: M3's goal is to scale to more objects and languages with sustained quality. After the first partner delivery (delivery-001), additional objects from the same or a second partner museum must be produced in source language and translated into ≥2 target languages. Reviewer rotation (rotation-001) and outcome tracking (outcomes-001) must be active. All pipeline gates (readability, grounding, license-002, qualified reviewer) apply exactly as in M2.
objective: Produce and deliver additional plain-language object descriptions beyond the initial ≥25, translating each into ≥2 agreed target languages, using the full M1 pipeline (license-002, watcher-001, glossary per language pair, escalation-001 procedure, reviewer-rotation roster). The resulting set should bring total adopted objects to ≥25 across ≥2 target languages, or onboard a second partner museum.
acceptanceCriteria: Additional objects beyond the initial M2 set produced with reviewed source descriptions, each passing the full pipeline: sourced facts, readability gate, grounding check, domain-aware reviewer sign-off, license-002 CI green
Each additional object translated into ≥2 target languages, each translation passing qualified bilingual reviewer sign-off and license-002 CI check
Reviewer rotation (rotation-001) used: no single reviewer dominates the entire batch; load balancing documented
All escalated items (sensitive-heritage, contested-legal) handled per escalation-001 procedure; no ungated material delivered
Total adopted objects reach ≥25 across ≥2 target languages (or a second partner museum onboarded), confirmed by partner display/publication evidence
Outcome-tracking log (outcomes-001) updated with adoption status, any factual defects, and partner/visitor feedback for the new batch
output: objects/<objectId>/descriptions/<lang>.md across additional objects in ≥2 target languages; updated project registry with adoption evidence; updated outcomes-001 log
